headline: Dream Exchange Highlights Investor Cory Knox
summary: Cory Knox Cory Knox is a consumer packaged goods professional and investor in Dream Exchange. CHICAGO, June 04, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Dream Exchange is pleased to highlight Cory Knox as a participant in our investor community. As an organization funded entirely by individual investors, Dream Exchange is proud to showcase the wide-ranging professional backgrounds of our support base. Cory Knox brings a remarkable level of operational excellence and supply chain expertise to our growing network

headline: How to Maximize Dividend Income in Retirement Before RMDs Change the Math
summary: At the 24% federal bracket, a portfolio throwing off $40,000 in high-yield dividend income hands roughly $9,600 to the IRS every year when those shares sit in a taxable account treated as ordinary income. For investors in the gap years between retirement and RMD age 73, that drag compounds quietly until required minimum distributions force ... How to Maximize Dividend Income in Retirement Before RMDs Change the Math

headline: Without a Roth, $60,000 in Dividend Income From These 5 Stocks Means You Owe the IRS $22,200
summary: At the 37% top federal bracket, a portfolio throwing off $60,000 in non-qualified dividend income hands the IRS $22,200 every April, before state taxes and before the 3.8% net investment income tax (NIIT) surtax that also applies at this income level. That is the cost of holding REITs, BDCs and other ordinary-income dividend payers in ... Without a Roth, $60,000 in Dividend Income From These 5 Stocks Means You Owe the IRS $22,200

headline: P&G to Webcast Presentation From the Evercore Consumer and Retail Conference, June 10
summary: CINCINNATI, June 04, 2026--Seth Cohen, Chief Information Officer of The Procter & Gamble Company (NYSE:PG) will be a featured speaker at the 6th Annual Evercore Consumer and Retail Conference on Wednesday, June 10, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. ET.

headline: Why Social Security’s 2.8% COLA Isn’t Keeping Up With What Retirees Actually Spend on Healthcare
summary: The Social Security Administration (SSA) set the 2026 cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) at 2.8%, lifting the average retired worker’s benefit from $2,015 to $2,071 per month. That extra $56 sounds fine on paper. Then the Medicare Part B premium notice arrives, the Medigap renewal letter shows up, and the math gets tighter than expected. One retiree ... Why Social Security’s 2.8% COLA Isn’t Keeping Up With What Retirees Actually Spend on Healthcare

headline: Can Pure Dividend Stocks Replace a $110,000 Dual-Income Household Income? Here’s What It Would Take
summary: A combined household income of $110,000 is close to the national norm for a two-earner household. For a 56-year-old couple hoping to retire at age 60 and fund their lifestyle entirely through dividend income, that annual amount becomes the income target their portfolio must replace. The basic calculation is straightforward: divide the desired income by ... Can Pure Dividend Stocks Replace a $110,000 Dual-Income Household Income? Here’s What It Would Take

headline: Procter & Gamble (PG) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Softness
summary: Why Procter & Gamble stock is in focus Procter & Gamble (PG) is back on investors’ radar after recent share price softness, with the stock down over the past week, month and past 3 months, prompting fresh attention on its fundamentals. See our latest analysis for Procter & Gamble. That short term share price weakness, including a 7 day share price return of 4.95% and a 90 day share price return of 8.96% in the red, sits against a mixed longer record.
